# Changelog

All notable changes to ddx-agent are recorded here.
Dates use the repo convention (`YYYY-MM-DD`); versions follow semver.

## [Unreleased]

## [v0.9.24] — 2026-04-28

Two harness-parity blockers surfaced by the first agent-vs-pi paired
beadbench run.

### Fixed

- **Pi-runner panic on `send on closed channel`** (`agent-195bb183`).
  The `mirroredEvents` goroutine in `internal/harnesses/pi/runner.go`
  was leaked: nothing closed the intermediate channel after
  `parsePiStream` returned, so an in-flight event could race
  `run()`'s deferred `close(out)` and panic. Fix: `mirroredEvents`
  now reports ownership of the mid-channel; the parser-goroutine
  closes it and waits for the mirror goroutine to drain before
  signaling done. Added `TestRunner_Execute_MirrorClosesCleanly`,
  passes 20× under `-race`.
- **Test-config isolation in `agent_test`** (`agent-27806ad5`). Tests
  calling `agent.New(ServiceOptions{})` auto-loaded the developer's
  live `~/.config/agent/config.yaml`, including provider types older
  base revisions didn't know about — making bead-verify-worktrees
  fail on `go test ./...` for reasons unrelated to the bead. New
  package-level `TestMain` (`service_testmain_test.go`) points
  `HOME` and `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` at a fresh tempdir before `m.Run()`.

# Changelog

All notable changes to ddx-agent are recorded here.
Dates use the repo convention (`YYYY-MM-DD`); versions follow semver.

## [Unreleased]

## [v0.9.24] — 2026-04-28

Two harness-parity blockers surfaced by the first agent-vs-pi paired
beadbench run.

### Fixed

- **Pi-runner panic on `send on closed channel`** (`agent-195bb183`).
  The `mirroredEvents` goroutine in `internal/harnesses/pi/runner.go`
  was leaked: nothing closed the intermediate channel after
  `parsePiStream` returned, so an in-flight event could race
  `run()`'s deferred `close(out)` and panic. Fix: `mirroredEvents`
  now reports ownership of the mid-channel; the parser-goroutine
  closes it and waits for the mirror goroutine to drain before
  signaling done. Added `TestRunner_Execute_MirrorClosesCleanly`,
  passes 20× under `-race`.
- **Test-config isolation in `agent_test`** (`agent-27806ad5`). Tests
  calling `agent.New(ServiceOptions{})` auto-loaded the developer's
  live `~/.config/agent/config.yaml`, including provider types older
  base revisions didn't know about — making bead-verify-worktrees
  fail on `go test ./...` for reasons unrelated to the bead. New
  package-level `TestMain` (`service_testmain_test.go`) points
  `HOME` and `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` at a fresh tempdir before `m.Run()`.
  Per-test `t.Setenv("HOME", …)` calls still take precedence.

## [v0.9.23] — 2026-04-28

External-benchmark integration. First adapter to compare our harness
against the public scoreboard.

### Added

- **TerminalBench adapter** (`agent-6d6ae2f6`, PR #4). New
  `internal/benchmark/external/termbench/` package that translates
  TerminalBench task format → `agent.ServiceExecuteRequest` and
  captures `harnesses.Event` streams into ATIF v1.4 trajectory output
  the upstream verifier expects. Reads upstream `reward.txt` without
  reimplementing grading — the value of an external benchmark is
  using their rubric.
- Submodule at `scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2/` pinned
  to upstream commit `53ff2b8`. (Bead originally pointed at
  `laude-institute/terminal-bench`; that's stale per SD-008 — current
  dataset lives at terminal-bench-2.)
- Frozen 20-task subset at
  `scripts/beadbench/external/termbench-subset.json` (15 stratified
  hard/medium tasks reused from the evidence-grade comparison + 5
  easy smoke tasks).
- `cmd/bench --external=termbench` flag plus
  `cmd/bench/external_termbench.go` runner. Writes results to
  `benchmark-results/termbench/<run-id>/results.json`.
- `docs/helix/02-design/external-benchmarks.md` — pattern doc for
  adding more external benchmark adapters (SWE-bench, AgentBench,
  etc.) following the TerminalBench shape.
- `docs/research/external-benchmark-baseline-2026-04-27.md` —
  baseline matrix scaffold (3 tasks × 3 arms: ddx-agent on
  Qwen3.6-plus, codex on GPT-5.4, claude-code on Sonnet-4.6). Cells
  marked `_pending_` until the verifier-side run lands.

### Honestly-scoped v1 gaps

- The adapter does not invoke Harbor / pytest / Docker. It writes
  the artifacts the verifier expects; actual scoring remains
  `scripts/benchmark/run_benchmark.sh`'s job.
- Baseline doc cells are placeholders pending verifier-side run.

## [v0.9.22] — 2026-04-28

Refactor + reliability release. Closes the architectural smell that
caused v0.9.18-.20's lucebox/vllm shipping bug, lays down the
internal-benchmark-corpus foundation, and unblocks lucebox conformance
on thinking-mode locals.

### Refactor

- **Provider registry** (`agent-8e4eb44c`). Single source of truth for
  provider-type → factory mappings in `internal/provider/registry/`.
  Each provider package registers a Descriptor in `init()`. The two
  parallel factories (`internal/config/config.go:buildProviderFromConfig`
  and `service_native_provider.go:buildNativeProvider`) collapse to
  a `registry.Lookup(...)` + `Factory(Inputs{...})`. 11 types
  registered (anthropic, lmstudio, lucebox, minimax, ollama, omlx,
  openai, openrouter, qwen, vllm, zai). Round-trip test in
  `registry_test.go` enumerates expected types and asserts every one
  resolves and produces a non-nil provider — catches drift on import.
  Independent fresh-eyes review verified no information loss.

### Added

- **Internal benchmark corpus + curation flow** (bead `agent-4f03f33d`).
  New `internal/corpus` package + `ddx-agent corpus
  promote|validate|list` subcommand stand up a capability-tagged
  corpus of closed beads worth regression-tracking. Files live under
  `scripts/beadbench/corpus.yaml`, `scripts/beadbench/corpus/<bead-id>.yaml`,
  and `scripts/beadbench/corpus/capabilities.yaml` (controlled
  vocabulary). `run_beadbench.py` learned `--corpus-only` and
  `--capability=<tag>` filters. Promotion is intentionally manual —
  curation is the value. See
  `docs/helix/02-design/benchmark-corpus.md` for the curation rules.
  Seeded with three beads: `agent-39f79181` (sampling resolver CLI
  wiring), `agent-b6b15fb0` (openai-flavor thinking-leak — failure
  mode), `agent-37aeb88e` (beadbench harness instrumentation).

### Fixed

- **Conformance prompt brittleness for thinking-mode locals**
  (`agent-bcea2d77`). Replaced literal-substring assertions
  ("pong" / "stream-pong" / "reasoning") with structural checks
  (non-empty content + finish_reason). Literal-substring assertions
  remain available as opt-in for shaped-double fixtures.
  `streaming_max_tokens_honored` cap floors at 256 for
  `SupportsThinking=true` providers since the 3-token default is
  incompatible with reasoning_content emission. Live conformance
  against lucebox should pass the streaming chat tests once the
  upstream `tool_choice` fix lands (separate report).
- **CI route-status flake** (`agent-901cd778`). Two cmd/agent
  route-status tests asserted every candidate has a non-empty
  Model field. Subscription-harness candidates (claude/codex/gemini)
  appear ineligible with empty Model in clean CI environments
  (their CLI binaries aren't on PATH). Tightened: only Eligible
  candidates must name a resolved model.

### Documentation

- `docs/research/qwen3.6-27b-cross-provider-2026-04-27.md` — Tier-3
  beadbench results added. Tier-2 single-prompt grading was 100%
  on every target; Tier-3 multi-turn agent loop revealed local
  27B is not yet production-viable (both vidar omlx 8bit and
  bragi LM Studio timed out at 1 hour on a real bead while
  openrouter qwen3.6-plus finished in 4.5 min, $0.32).
- `docs/research/lucebox-tool-support-2026-04-27.md` extended
  with Gap 3 (Blackwell-consumer sm_120 perf unswept) and Gap 4
  (server stability under burst load).

## [v0.9.21] — 2026-04-27

Bug-fix release. Closes the lucebox + vllm provider integration loop
that v0.9.18-.20 left half-shipped, and adds deeper tool-call
conformance coverage that surfaced the gap.

### Fixed

- `service_native_provider.go`'s `buildNativeProvider` had its own
  switch over provider types parallel to `internal/config/config.go`'s
  factory — the lucebox + vllm additions in v0.9.18-.20 missed it.
  Result: `ServiceExecuteRequest` with `provider=lucebox` or `=vllm`
  failed at execute time with `no configured provider matches type`
  even though config validation accepted them. Both factories now
  register both types. Filed as architectural smell `agent-8e4eb44c`
  (provider registry refactor) for the longer-term fix.

### Added

- **Conformance tool-call coverage doubled** (commit `72399fa`).
  Existing `tool_call_streaming` subtest tightened — args must parse as
  JSON with non-empty `target` string, not just contain the literal.
  Three new subtests under `SupportsToolCalls`:
  - `non-streaming_tool_call` — exercises Chat path independently of
    ChatStream so regressions in either are visible.
  - `multi-tool_wire_shape` — sends 3 tools in one request; asserts
    response chose one of them (does not lock to which — that's
    model intelligence, not wire conformance).
  - `tool_result_roundtrip` — synthesizes user → assistant tool_call →
    tool result conversation; asserts the follow-up does not re-emit
    a tool call. Validates `tool_call_id` pairing serialization.
- Two new conformance helper tools: `summarizeTool()`, `countWordsTool()`.
- Shaped-double fixtures for both wire flavors (openai-compat,
  anthropic) updated to support the new subtests — non-streaming with
  tools returns tool_calls; tool-result history returns plain content.

### Documentation

- `docs/research/lucebox-tool-support-2026-04-27.md` — comprehensive
  report on the lucebox-hub dflash server's wire compliance + four
  gaps (tool_choice silently ignored, conservative auto-mode,
  Blackwell-consumer perf unswept, server stability under burst load).
- `docs/research/qwen3.6-27b-cross-provider-2026-04-27.md` — Tier-2
  grading-harness comparison across openrouter (cloud baseline),
  vidar omlx, grendel omlx, bragi lucebox, bragi LM Studio. All six
  targets pass 8/8 quality; 23× spread on speed. Identifies bragi
  LM Studio as the production local pick today.



Renames `luce` → `lucebox` (aligns with upstream lucebox-hub project,
removes ambiguity with "Luce" the org), promotes the provider to
Thinking=true (server returns Qwen3-style reasoning_content), and
adds per-Capabilities knobs to the conformance suite so thinking-mode
local providers can be exercised with appropriate headroom.

### Renamed (breaking for existing config users)

- Provider package `internal/provider/luce/` → `internal/provider/lucebox/`.
- Provider type string `"luce"` → `"lucebox"` everywhere (config

520:ADR-006 chain ships: manual overrides become first-class auto-routing
521-failure signals. Plus the codex-design reasoning-variants chain
522-(catalog fields → provider-wire enforcement → auto-resolution),
523-selection-precedence spec amendments, and the AGENTS.md discipline
524-sections that operationalize this session's learnings.
525-
526-### Added
527-
528-- **Override-as-failure-signal telemetry (ADR-006).** `Execute` now
529-  emits `override` and `rejected_override` events when callers pin any
530-  of `Harness`/`Provider`/`Model`. The override event captures both the
531-  user-pinned decision and the unconstrained auto-decision (computed
532-  via a second in-process `ResolveRoute` with override axes stripped),
533:  so future routing improvements have data on where users disagree
534-  with the optimizer. Per-axis tracking; coincidental agreement still
535-  fires the event. (`agent-9fc2633c`, `agent-017b043f`,
536-  `agent-79aedde2`)
537-- **Routing-quality metrics on `RouteStatus` and `UsageReport`.**
538-  `auto_acceptance_rate`, `override_disagreement_rate`, and
539-  `override_class_breakdown` exposed as first-class metrics distinct
540-  from per-(provider, model) provider reliability. UsageReport reads
541-  from session logs over the `--since` window; RouteStatus uses the
542-  in-memory ring. Both honor the new metric-vs-reliability separation
543-  in their UI. (`agent-017b043f`)
544-- **`ddx agent route-status --overrides --since DURATION` operator
545-  surface.** Prints the override-class breakdown over the selected
546-  window, with `--axis harness|provider|model` filter and `--json`
547-  output. Help text references ADR-006. (`agent-79aedde2`)
548-- **Catalog reasoning capability fields.** `ModelEntry` gains
549-  `reasoning_levels []string`, `reasoning_control string`
550-  (`tunable | fixed | none`), and `reasoning_wire string`
551-  (`provider | model_id | none`). Eight manifest entries populated
552-  covering all three control/wire cases. Captures the codex-design
553-  distinction between tunable-effort and reasoning-variant models.
554-  (`agent-a0160cde`)
555-- **`Reasoning=auto` resolves to surface-policy default before
556-  capability gate.** Routing engine looks up the target's
557-  `reasoning_default` for the requested tier and uses it as the
558-  resolved level for gating. Empty/unset `Reasoning` still bypasses
559-  the gate. Forward-protection against future non-thinking variants
560-  landing in higher tiers. (`agent-ba467996`)
561-- **Spec selection-precedence section in CONTRACT-003.** Documents
562-  pin precedence (Harness → Provider → Model → ModelRef → Profile)
563-  with explicit hard/soft semantics and Profile's role as
--
572:- **AR-2026-04-25-routing-and-overrides alignment review.** Captures
573:  the v0.9.9 / v0.9.10 / ADR-006 narrative through-line with seven
574-  findings driving the discipline updates.

576-### Fixed
577-
578-- **`rejected_override` events now persist to session log.** Pre-
579-  dispatch validation failures (orphan model, unknown provider) emit
580-  rejected_override events to both the channel and the session log so
581-  UsageReport's window aggregation sees them. Prior implementation
582-  broadcast-only, missing the persistent record. (`6c4340f`)
583-- **Provider-wire enforcement for fixed-variant models.** The
584-  openai-compat dispatch consults the resolved model's
585-  `reasoning_wire` field. `model_id` mode strips the reasoning field
586-  on the wire (model name is the encoding); `none` mode rejects
587-  pre-flight when `Reasoning` is non-off. Closes a silent
588-  OpenRouter-emits-reasoning-for-fixed-variant-Qwen bug codex flagged.
589-  (`agent-b6b15fb0`)
590-- **Pi+provider-pin model validation duplicated on engine path.** The
591:  routing engine's `pinValidation` rejected pi+qwen with
592:  `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` even though `service_execute` had a
593-  bypass for pi-with-explicit-provider. Mirrored the bypass in the
594-  engine. (`605729c`)
595-- **`TestListHarnesses_GeminiAccountAndUsageWindows` UTC-midnight
596-  flake.** Test now uses an injected deterministic clock; passes 10/10
597-  in repeat without time manipulation. (`0b22784`)
598-
599-### Changed
600-
601-- **Selection-precedence section in CONTRACT-003 demoted to
602-  implementation reference.** Pin precedence prose stays accurate but
603-  is no longer presented as the primary user-facing surface. ADR-006
604-  reframes pins as override hatches; routine pinning for a given
605:  prompt class is a routing-quality issue to file. (`9a04ad4`,
606-  `c687caf`)
607-
608:## [v0.9.10] — 2026-04-25
609-
610-Prompt-caching support lands across the public surface, the Anthropic provider,
611-the openai-compat regression gate, and cost attribution. No breaking changes.
612-
613-### Added
614-
615-- **`ServiceExecuteRequest.CachePolicy` and `RouteRequest.CachePolicy`.**
616-  Public opt-out for callers who must disable caching (deterministic eval,
617-  privacy-sensitive prompts, one-shot benchmark runs). Values: `""` and
618-  `"default"` mean "cache as designed"; `"off"` suppresses provider-side
619-  cache markers and emits explicit-zero cache-amounts in cost attribution.
620-  Unknown values rejected at the boundary. (`agent-cccc2df7`)
621-- **Anthropic provider emits `cache_control: {type: "ephemeral"}` on the
622-  last tool definition and the last system block.** Multi-turn native
623-  Anthropic sessions now hit Anthropic's prompt cache (~10× discount on
624-  cache reads). System-block construction extracted to a shared
625-  `buildSystemBlocks` helper consumed by both `Chat` and `ChatStream` so
626-  streaming and non-streaming paths cache identically. `CachePolicy="off"`
627-  suppresses both markers. Wire-body assertions cover both paths.
628-  (`agent-3bc67e94`)
629-- **openai-compat prefix-stability regression gate.** New wire-level test
630-  via `httptest.NewServer` captures actual HTTP request bodies across two
631-  `Chat` calls with identical tools+system but a differing trailing user
632-  message; asserts byte-equality on the prefix. A negative test
633-  (`TestOpenAIRequestPreservesToolOrder`) ensures tool order is preserved.
634-  No behavior change — this protects auto-prefix-caching on OpenAI,
635-  LM Studio, oMLX, and OpenRouter against silent regressions.
636-  (`agent-50658a65`)
637-- **Cache-aware cost attribution at the native loop.** `core.ModelPricing`
638-  carries `CacheReadPerM` / `CacheWritePerM`; `modelcatalog.PricingFor`
--
648:  routing test expectations.
649-- **Architecture doc gains a Caching section.** Documents the prefix-order
650-  invariant (tools → system → conversation → trailing user), two-marker
651-  placement, and the compaction and tool-mutation caveats.
652-
653-### Changed
654-
655-- Existing telemetry parsing of `cache_read_input_tokens`,
656-  `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`
657-  is unchanged but now feeds cost attribution that actually uses those
658-  numbers. Operators reading `ddx-agent usage` get accurate cost figures
659-  for cache-supporting providers.
660-
661-### Fixed
662-
663-- **Cache-amount nil-vs-zero ambiguity in cost attribution.** Reviewer
664-  caught that the initial cost-attribution wiring set
665-  `CacheReadAmount` / `CacheWriteAmount` pointers unconditionally during
666-  configured-cost computation, collapsing two distinct conventions:
667-  `CachePolicy="off"` (explicit zero) and "harness reports zero cache
668-  tokens" (nil). Fixed in `6cdfdc5`; the nil leg is now covered by
669-  `TestCacheAttributionNilWhenHarnessReportsZeroAndPolicyDefault`.
670-
671-## [v0.9.9] — 2026-04-25
672-
673:This release lands ADR-005 (smart routing replaces `model_routes`) plus the
674-preceding service-boundary work that made it possible. The agent now picks
675-`(harness, provider, model)` automatically from the catalog, configured
676-provider liveness, and per-(provider, model) signal — no
677-`model_routes:` YAML required. Native Anthropic still does not write
678-`cache_control`; that work is staged separately.
679-
680-### Breaking
681-
682-- **`ServiceExecuteRequest.PreResolved` removed.** Callers no longer round-trip
683:  a `RouteDecision` from `ResolveRoute` into `Execute`. `ResolveRoute` is
684-  informational only; `Execute` always re-resolves on its own inputs.
685-  (`agent-ddcc903b`, ADR-005)
686-- **`ServiceConfig.ModelRouteConfig` / `ModelRouteNames` removed from the
687-  primary config surface.** Legacy `model_routes:` YAML still parses for one
688-  release with a deprecation warning that names the offending key path; the
689-  next release rejects it outright. The deprecation surface lives in
690-  `internal/config/legacy_model_routes.go`. A boundary test forbids
691-  reintroduction in `internal/config/config.go`. (`agent-21a521fc`, ADR-005)
692:- **`cmd/agent/routing_provider.go` `routeProvider` type and
693-  `cmd/agent/provider_build.go` deleted.** Provider construction and
694:  per-Chat failover are owned by the service-side smart routing engine.
695-  Route-status display helpers remain. (`agent-21a521fc`)
696-
697-### Added
698-
699:- **Smart routing auto-selection inputs.** `ServiceExecuteRequest` and
700-  `RouteRequest` carry `EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools`. When the
701-  caller pins nothing, the service filters candidates by context window and
702-  tool support before scoring. Explicit `--profile` / `--model` /

487	## [v0.9.12] — 2026-04-26
488	
489	Operational/research release — scaffolds the harness-parity iteration loop
490	that compares native agent vs pi on shared backings. Benchmark execution
491	itself is in progress at tag time (long-running; ~3-8h wall-clock); results
492	+ winner declaration + top-3 gaps land in v0.9.13.
493	
494	### Added
495	
496	- **Beadbench arms for harness-parity comparison** on the same omlx vidar
497	  Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit backing, both with codex/gpt-5.5 reviewer pin:
498	  `agent-omlx-vidar-qwen36` (native agent harness) and
499	  `pi-omlx-vidar-qwen36` (pi harness via pi's anthropic-messages
500	  config to vidar omlx). Pairs in the manifest so the runner produces
501	  apples-to-apples comparison output. (`agent-bea748e7`)
502	- **Research AR scaffolding for the parity benchmark.**
503	  `docs/research/AR-2026-04-26-agent-vs-pi-omlx-vidar-qwen36.md` lands
504	  with sections 1 (methodology — paired design, N≥8, reviewer pin, win
505	  condition, tiebreaker) and 2 (pi-config evidence — the trailing-comma
506	  JSON syntax fix and the openai-completions → anthropic-messages
507	  switch that unblocked pi against omlx). Sections 3–5 (per-task
508	  pairwise table, aggregate metrics, top-3 gaps) marked TBD pending the
509	  benchmark run. (`agent-1bffdd79`)
510	
511	### Changed
512	
513	- **Harness-parity bead split into four children** matching the
514	  iteration-loop structure: manifest arms (filed v0.9.12), doc skeleton
515	  (filed v0.9.12), benchmark execution (in-flight at tag time), reactive
516	  iteration tweak beads (filed after data lands).
517	
518	## [v0.9.11] — 2026-04-26
519	
520	ADR-006 chain ships: manual overrides become first-class auto-routing
521	failure signals. Plus the codex-design reasoning-variants chain

input.content:
# No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

## Decision

Stopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a
multi-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it
in one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that
fails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config
schema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs
without the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.

## What is already done (verified in this worktree)

1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under
   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in
   `closed` status:
     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)
     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)
     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)
     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)
     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)

2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**
   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns
   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)
   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1
   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.

## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass

### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative

AC #1 asks for "a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class
default and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +
gating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost
dimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback."

Reading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:
  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.
  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.
  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,
    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent
    "routing point release" narrative.

The closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as
side-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the
combined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires
either (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a "v0.9.25 — routing
point release" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead
description must be relaxed to accept "the code is in v0.9.23, even if
no single CHANGELOG block summarizes it." Either resolution is a
governance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.

### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)

These criteria collectively require:
  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one
    `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call with no Model / no Harness /
    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;
  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim
    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);
  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,
    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,
    with one-time process warnings;
  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to
    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;
  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /
    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with
    dead-code detection in CI;
  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn
    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override
    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness
    conflict);
  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's
    real config;
  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.

This is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:
  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`
  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)
  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)
  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)
  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)

That work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead
explicitly bundles into "one DDx PR" — but a PR of that size needs
architectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking
config change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the
first line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC
deterministically, not to make architecture decisions.

### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads

The bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:
  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,
  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)
    stays independent.

Filing beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the
project's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is
mechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.

### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden

A net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical
19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test
(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not
have gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

To make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead
must be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:

1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever
   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add
   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially
   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.

2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default
   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:
   "default")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.

3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before
   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded
   incompatibility.

4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on
   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +
   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.

5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override
   combination.

6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test
   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.

7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of
   compensating logic.

8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.

9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI
   bead.

Each of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The
current bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one
attempt.

## Recommendation to the orchestrator

Reopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into
the nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three
(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated
goals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") suggests
this bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as
the deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.

data.text:
Stopping without a commit. Wrote `.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining why.

Summary: this bead is an EPIC describing 9+ distinct deliverables (go.mod bump, default-path refactor, preflight gate, breaking config deprecation, override e2e tests, frontend Playwright goldens, dead-code cleanup, regression test, follow-up bead filing, and CHANGELOG/docs). Verified upstream routing-plan beads (`agent-0dafc7f0`, `agent-191a74f9`, `agent-1f46cf22`, `agent-53f38d95`, `agent-bab52778`, `agent-dfabb10b`) are all closed and their commits are transitively in the v0.9.23 tag DDx already pulls — but no single upstream tag's CHANGELOG narrates them as a "routing point release" the way AC #1 demands, and the DDx-side D1-D5 work spans ~1-3k LOC across dispatch, config schema, CLI flags, and tests with breaking changes that need architectural sign-off. Recommended splitting into nine sub-beads.
